title: caucalis platycarpos var. muricata
intro: Caucalis platycarpos var. muricata, also known as Bertram's buttonweed or muricate caucalis, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, commonly found in Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Caucalis platycarpos var. muricata include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and aid in digestion, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or using them as a tincture, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or applied topically for skin issues.
